subject: An ancient stone shrine in a sunlit jungle clearing, with a shaft of golden light descending onto the altar
description: A weathered stone shrine or altar sits at the center of a deep jungle clearing, framed by towering tree trunks and craggy rock formations on either side. A vertical shaft of warm golden light pierces the canopy and falls directly onto the shrine, suggesting a sacred or numinous quality. Dense green foliage, vines, and ferns crowd the foreground and middle distance, while the background opens to misty, light-bathed depths of the forest. The composition is hushed and reverent, with no figures present — the shrine itself is the protagonist.
foreground: stone shrine on a raised dais, surrounded by ferns, vines, and undergrowth
background: towering jungle trees and rock walls fading into golden mist and distant canopy
emotion: still, reverent, awestruck — the empty shrine reads as long-abandoned but still holy
